item.title: GIS as the System of Record for ADMS and OMS
item.description:
GIS as the System of Record for ADMS and OMS

Utilities rarely fail ADMS or OMS projects because of software. They fail because foundational GIS data isn’t ready.

Why GIS Is Central

GIS defines:

  • Network connectivity
  • Phase modeling
  • Operating voltage
  • Asset relationships

Every downstream system depends on this accuracy.

Common Data Gaps

  • Self-intersecting lines
  • Missing or invalid phase designation
  • Null facility IDs
  • Inconsistent feeder modeling

What Actually Works

A targeted GIS data readiness assessment before OMS or ADMS implementation or upgrades.

item.title: GIS in the Center: The Architecture Utilities Actually Operate On
item.description:
GIS in the Center: The Architecture Utilities Actually Operate On

Utilities often talk about “integrated systems,” but in practice, most integrations already revolve around one platform:

GIS.

Not as a mapping tool—but as the operational system of record that defines how the grid actually exists.

The Reality of Utility Architecture

Modern utilities run a complex ecosystem of systems:

  • OMS
  • ADMS / DMS
  • SCADA
  • DERMS
  • Engineering Analysis
  • Distribution Planning
  • Asset Management (EAM / APM)
  • Mobile Workforce / Field Mobility
  • Work Management
  • CIS
  • AMI / MDMS

On architecture diagrams, these systems are often drawn in neat layers or point-to-point integrations.

Articles

A collection of writing on utility GIS, data readiness, and operational system integration.

item.title: About
item.description:
About

I’m Joe Marsh, a senior consultant and product leader focused on helping utilities modernize operations through GIS, ADMS, utility network, and grid management solutions.

My work sits at the intersection of technology, strategy, and operations. I’m especially interested in how strong network data, clear system ownership, and practical architecture decisions improve reliability, planning, and operational performance.

Over the course of my career, I’ve worked on utility transformation efforts involving ADMS implementations, Utility Network migrations, outage management improvements, GIS data modeling, training, and business intelligence. I care most about turning complex technical environments into clear, usable outcomes for the people who operate them every day.
